In [154]:
import pandas as pd
import glob
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import calendar
In [155]:
files = glob.glob('/content/*.csv')   # all CSVs in /content/

df = pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f) for f in files], ignore_index=True)
print("total records:",len(df))
total records: 1861108

df.info()

Data Cleaning
In [158]:
df.columns = df.columns.str.lower().str.strip().str.replace(" ", "_")
df

In [159]:
df = df.dropna()
 
Fix state name inconsistencies
In [160]:
df["state"] = (
    df["state"]
    .str.lower()
    .str.strip()
    .str.replace(r"\s+", " ", regex=True)
)
 
Create a MASTER STATE MAPPING
In [161]:
state_mapping = {
    # West Bengal variations
    "west  bengal": "west bengal",
    "westbengal": "west bengal",
    "west bangal": "west bengal",
    "west bengli": "west bengal",
    "jammu & kashmir": "jammu and kashmir",
    "andaman and nicobar islands": "andaman and nicobar",
    "daman & diu": "dadra and nagar haveli and daman and diu",
    "dadra & nagar haveli": "dadra and nagar haveli and daman and diu",
    "dadra and nagar haveli": "dadra and nagar haveli and daman and diu",
    "pondicherry": "puducherry",
    "chhatisgarh": "chhattisgarh",
    "uttaranchal": "uttarakhand"

}
In [162]:
df["state"] = df["state"].replace(state_mapping)
 
REMOVE NON-STATE ENTRIES
In [163]:
valid_states = {
    "andhra pradesh","arunachal pradesh","assam","bihar","chhattisgarh",
    "goa","gujarat","haryana","himachal pradesh","jharkhand","karnataka",
    "kerala","madhya pradesh","maharashtra","manipur","meghalaya","mizoram",
    "nagaland","odisha","punjab","rajasthan","sikkim","tamil nadu",
    "telangana","tripura","uttar pradesh","uttarakhand","west bengal",
    "andaman and nicobar","chandigarh","dadra and nagar haveli and daman and diu",
    "delhi","jammu and kashmir","ladakh","lakshadweep","puducherry"
}
df = df[df["state"].isin(valid_states)]
 
VERIFY CLEANING SUCCESS
In [164]:
print("Unique Clean States:", df["state"].nunique())
print(sorted(df["state"].unique()))

Now created a new column for differentiating states and union territories
In [165]:
states = {
    "andhra pradesh","arunachal pradesh","assam","bihar","chhattisgarh",
    "goa","gujarat","haryana","himachal pradesh","jharkhand","karnataka",
    "kerala","madhya pradesh","maharashtra","manipur","meghalaya","mizoram",
    "nagaland","odisha","punjab","rajasthan","sikkim","tamil nadu",
    "telangana","tripura","uttar pradesh","uttarakhand","west bengal"
}

union_territories = {
    "andaman and nicobar",
    "chandigarh",
    "dadra and nagar haveli and daman and diu",
    "delhi",
    "jammu and kashmir",
    "ladakh",
    "lakshadweep",
    "puducherry"
}
 
ADD A NEW COLUMN → region_type
In [166]:
def classify_region(state):
    if state in states:
        return "State"
    elif state in union_territories:
        return "Union Territory"
    else:
        return "Unknown"

df["region_type"] = df["state"].apply(classify_region)
In [167]:
df["region_type"].value_counts()
Out[167]:
count
region_type
State 1807706
Union Territory 39287

In [168]:
#created the timedataset for creating the time graphs and this is the cleaning process dtataset creatioon i have done for timedata set creation.

df_time = df.copy()

df_time["date"] = pd.to_datetime(df_time["date"], errors="coerce")

# keep only valid dates
df_time = df_time[df_time["date"].notna()]

print("Records with valid dates:", len(df_time))
Records with valid dates: 910122
 
In [169]:
df_time["year"] = df_time["date"].dt.year
df_time["month"] = df_time["date"].dt.month
 
created seperate database for state and union territories
In [170]:
#for state only
df_states = df[df["region_type"] == "State"]

#for union territories
df_uts = df[df["region_type"] == "Union Territory"]
 
#groupby ordering for the region wise
In [171]:
region_time = df_time.groupby(
    ["year", "region_type"]
)["bio_age_17_"].sum().reset_index()
In [172]:
df_time["year"] = df_time["date"].dt.year
df_time["month"] = df_time["date"].dt.month
 

Now Creating Region Wise Mapping

In [173]:
region_mapping = {
    # North
    "jammu and kashmir": "North",
    "ladakh": "North",
    "himachal pradesh": "North",
    "punjab": "North",
    "haryana": "North",
    "delhi": "North",
    "uttarakhand": "North",
    "uttar pradesh": "North",

    # South
    "tamil nadu": "South",
    "kerala": "South",
    "karnataka": "South",
    "andhra pradesh": "South",
    "telangana": "South",

    # West
    "rajasthan": "West",
    "gujarat": "West",
    "maharashtra": "West",
    "goa": "West",

    # East
    "west bengal": "East",
    "odisha": "East",
    "bihar": "East",
    "jharkhand": "East",

    # Central
    "madhya pradesh": "Central",
    "chhattisgarh": "Central",

    # North East
    "assam": "North-East",
    "arunachal pradesh": "North-East",
    "manipur": "North-East",
    "meghalaya": "North-East",
    "mizoram": "North-East",
    "nagaland": "North-East",
    "tripura": "North-East",

    # UT Islands
    "andaman and nicobar": "Islands",
    "lakshadweep": "Islands",
    "puducherry": "South"
}

        "High concentration in few states",
        "Transaction clustering in few districts",
        "Extreme concentration at pincode level"

Trend
0 Adult-dominant biometric usage
1 High concentration in few states

Pattern Discoveries
 
Pattern 1 — Concentration pattern

Few districts → most transactions
Few pincodes → majority within districts

charts regarding time
In [202]:
#monthly trend charts

monthly_trend = df_time.groupby(["year", "month"])[
    ["bio_age_5_17", "bio_age_17_"]
].sum().reset_index()
In [203]:
monthly_trend["year_month"] = monthly_trend["year"].astype(str) + '-' + monthly_trend["month"].astype(str).str.zfill(2)

plt.figure(figsize=(14,6))

plt.plot(
    monthly_trend["year_month"],
    monthly_trend["bio_age_17_"],
    label="Age 17+"
)

plt.plot(
    monthly_trend["year_month"],
    monthly_trend["bio_age_5_17"],
    label="Age 5–17"
)

plt.title("Monthly Trend of Aadhaar Biometric Usage (Valid Dates Only)")
plt.xlabel("Month-Year")
plt.ylabel("Total Biometric Transactions")
plt.xticks(rotation=90)
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
 
representing the biometric transactions change month-by-month
In [204]:
df_time["total_biometric"] = (
    df_time["bio_age_5_17"] + df_time["bio_age_17_"]
)

top10_districts = (
    df_time.groupby("district")["total_biometric"]
    .sum()
    .sort_values(ascending=False)
    .head(10)
    .index
)

# Monthly aggregating the top districts for the representation, now including 'year'
monthly_district = (
    df_time[df_time["district"].isin(top10_districts)]
    .groupby(["district", "year", "month"])["total_biometric"]
    .sum()
    .reset_index()
)
In [205]:
# Merge graph representation for top 10 Districts
# In this graph, it represents the biometric transactions change month-by-month for the top 10 districts.
plt.figure(figsize=(14,6))

# Create a 'year_month' column in monthly_district for consistent plotting
monthly_district["year_month"] = monthly_district["year"].astype(str) + '-' + monthly_district["month"].astype(str).str.zfill(2)

for district in top10_districts:
    data = monthly_district[monthly_district["district"] == district]
    plt.plot(
        data["year_month"],
        data["total_biometric"],
        marker="o",
        label=district
    )

plt.title("Monthly Biometric Trend – Top 10 Districts")
plt.xlabel("Month-Year")
plt.ylabel("Transactions")
plt.xticks(rotation=90) # Rotate for better readability
plt.legend(bbox_to_anchor=(1.05, 1), loc="upper left")
plt.grid(True)
plt.tight_layout() # Adjust layout to prevent labels from overlapping
plt.show()
In [216]:
# Load the education dataset
edu_df = pd.read_csv('/content/datafile.csv')

# Step 1: Standardize all column names to make them lowercase, strip spaces, and replace special characters
cleaned_columns = []
for col in edu_df.columns:
    # Convert to lowercase, strip leading/trailing whitespace
    cleaned_col = col.lower().strip()
    # Replace any sequences of non-alphanumeric characters with a single underscore
    # This handles " - " -> "_" and ensures consistency (e.g., no double underscores)
    cleaned_col = pd.Series(cleaned_col).str.replace(r'[^a-z0-9]+', '_', regex=True).iloc[0]
    # Remove leading/trailing underscores that might result from cleaning
    cleaned_col = cleaned_col.strip('_')
    cleaned_columns.append(cleaned_col)
edu_df.columns = cleaned_columns

# Step 2: Explicitly rename specific columns using their *newly cleaned* names as keys
edu_df = edu_df.rename(columns={
    "all_india_state_union_territory": "state", # Renamed from original header by general cleaning
    "2011__rural__person": "rural_literate_2011",   # Corrected key
    "2011__urban__persons": "urban_literate_2011"   # Corrected key
})

# Print info and head to confirm correct loading and renaming
print("edu_df loaded and columns processed:")
edu_df.info()
display(edu_df.head())

In [217]:
# Load the education dataset into edu_df
edu_df = pd.read_csv('/content/datafile.csv')

# Standardize all column names to make them lowercase, strip spaces, and replace special characters
edu_df.columns = (
    edu_df.columns
    .str.lower()
    .str.strip()
    .str.replace(" ", "_", regex=True)
    .str.replace("-", "")
)

# Rename specific columns that will be used for literacy calculation and state merging
edu_df = edu_df.rename(columns={
    "all_india/state/union_territory": "state",
    "2011__rural__person": "rural_literate_2011",
    "2011__urban__persons": "urban_literate_2011"
})

# Print info and head to confirm correct loading and renaming
print("edu_df loaded and columns processed:")
edu_df.info()
display(edu_df.head())

# Load the education dataset into edu_df
edu_df = pd.read_csv('/content/datafile.csv')

# Standardize all column names to make them lowercase, strip spaces, and replace special characters
edu_df.columns = (
    edu_df.columns
    .str.lower()
    .str.strip()
    .str.replace(" ", "_", regex=True)
    .str.replace("-", "")
)

# Rename specific columns that will be used for literacy calculation and state merging
edu_df = edu_df.rename(columns={
    "all_india/state/union_territory": "state",
    "2011__rural__person": "rural_literate_2011",
    "2011__urban__persons": "urban_literate_2011"
})

# Print info and head to confirm correct loading and renaming

In [208]:
# Calculate total literate population in edu_df
edu_df["total_literate_2011"] = (
    edu_df["rural_literate_2011"] +
    edu_df["urban_literate_2011"]
)

# Ensure state names in edu_df are clean and consistent for merging
edu_df["state"] = (
    edu_df["state"]
    .str.lower()
    .str.strip()
    .str.replace(r"\s+", " ", regex=True)
)
edu_df["state"] = edu_df["state"].replace(state_mapping)
edu_df = edu_df[edu_df["state"].isin(valid_states)]

print("edu_df after literate calculation and state cleaning:")
display(edu_df.head())

2 arunachal pradesh 42 52 30 54 64 44 67 52 60 88 77 83 143

In [213]:
# Merge child biometric data with education data
edu_merge = child_bio_state.merge(
    edu_df[["state", "total_literate_2011"]],
    on="state",
    how="inner"
)

print("Merged education and biometric data:")
display(edu_merge.head())

In [221]:
# Plot the scatter graph
plt.figure(figsize=(8,5))

plt.scatter(
    edu_merge["total_literate_2011"],
    edu_merge["bio_age_5_17"]
)

plt.xlabel("Total Literate Population (Census 2011)")
plt.ylabel("Child Biometric Transactions")
plt.title("Education (Literacy) vs Child Aadhaar Biometric Usage")
plt.grid(True)
plt.show()

In [238]:
# Create proper datetime column
monthly_district["date"] = pd.to_datetime(
    monthly_district["year"].astype(str) + "-" +
    monthly_district["month"].astype(str) + "-01"
)

# Sort dataset correctly
monthly_district = monthly_district.sort_values("date")

# Plot separate trend graph for top 10 districts
for district in top10_districts:
    data = monthly_district[monthly_district["district"] == district]

    plt.figure(figsize=(10,4))
    plt.plot(
        data["date"],
        data["total_biometric"],
        marker="o"
    )

    plt.title(f"Monthly Biometric Trend – {district}")
    plt.xlabel("Time")
    plt.ylabel("Biometric Transactions")
    plt.grid(True)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
 
HeatMap Representation
In [224]:
heatmap_data = monthly_district.pivot(
    index="district",
    columns="month",
    values="total_biometric"
)
 

HeatMap Visulaisation


The heatmap visualizes month-wise biometric intensity across top districts.
Darker regions indicate peak authentication periods, while lighter regions
represent relatively lower activity. Recurrent dark zones across districts
suggest system-wide seasonal behavior, whereas isolated spikes indicate localized anomalies.
In [225]:
plt.figure(figsize=(14,6))
plt.imshow(heatmap_data, aspect="auto")

plt.colorbar(label="Biometric Transactions")
plt.title("Monthly Biometric Intensity Heatmap – Top 10 Districts")

plt.xticks(
    ticks=range(12),
    labels=[calendar.month_abbr[i] for i in range(1,13)]
)

plt.yticks(
    ticks=range(len(heatmap_data.index)),
    labels=heatmap_data.index
)

plt.xlabel("Month")
plt.ylabel("District")
plt.show()
 
REGION-WISE BIOMETRIC COMPARISON
In [226]:
region_age = df.groupby("india_region")[[
    "bio_age_5_17",
    "bio_age_17_"
]].sum()
In [227]:
region_age.plot(kind="bar", figsize=(10,5))
plt.title("Region-wise Aadhaar Biometric Usage by Age Group")
plt.xlabel("India Region")
plt.ylabel("Total Transactions")
plt.show()
 
CHILD-FOCUSED ANALYSIS
In [228]:
df["child_share_percent"] = (
    df["bio_age_5_17"] /
    (df["bio_age_5_17"] + df["bio_age_17_"])
) * 100
child_region = df.groupby("india_region")["child_share_percent"].mean()
child_region
Out[228]:
child_share_percent
india_region
Central 43.606988
East 42.011077
Islands 58.925032
North 53.604450
North-East 56.650053
South 48.477113
West 44.049833

 
This visualization compares Aadhaar biometric usage across major Indian regions.
Adult biometric usage dominates in all regions; however, southern states show
relatively higher child biometric activity compared to northern and
north-eastern regions, indicating stronger integration of Aadhaar with education
and child-focused services.
In [229]:
region_age.plot(kind="bar", figsize=(10,5))
plt.title("Region-wise Aadhaar Biometric Usage by Age Group")
plt.xlabel("India Region")
plt.ylabel("Total Biometric Transactions")
plt.xticks(rotation=0)
plt.grid(True)
plt.show()
 

#

In [230]:
#Calculate child and adult share (%)
df["total_biometric"] = df["bio_age_5_17"] + df["bio_age_17_"]
df["child_share_percent"] = (
    df["bio_age_5_17"] / df["total_biometric"]
) * 100

df["adult_share_percent"] = (
    df["bio_age_17_"] / df["total_biometric"]
) * 100
 
Region Wise Average Share
In [231]:
region_share = df.groupby("india_region")[[
    "child_share_percent",
    "adult_share_percent"
]].mean()

region_share

child_share_percent adult_share_percent

Islands 58.925032 41.074968

South 48.477113 51.522887
West 44.049833 55.950167

region_share.plot(kind="bar", figsize=(10,5))
plt.title("Region-wise Child vs Adult Biometric Share")
plt.xlabel("India Region")
plt.ylabel("Average Share (%)")
plt.xticks(rotation=0)
plt.grid(True)
plt.show()
 
Education DataSet Load and using to compare with data
In [235]:
data=pd.read_csv('/content/cleaned_dataset.csv', on_bad_lines='skip')
data.info()
<class 'pandas.core.frame.DataFrame'> RangeIndex: 151534 entries, 0 to 151533 Data columns (total 8 columns): # Column Non-Null Count Dtype --- ------ -------------- ----- 0 date 151534 non-null object 1 state 151534 non-null object 2 district 151534 non-null object 3 pincode 151534 non-null int64 4 bio_age_5_17 151533 non-null float64 5 bio_age_17_ 151533 non-null float64 6 region_type 151533 non-null object 7 india_region 151111 non-null object dtypes: float64(2), int64(1), object(5) memory usage: 9.2+ MB
In [236]:
# Convert 'date' column to datetime, coercing errors to NaT (Not a Time)
data['date'] = pd.to_datetime(data['date'], errors='coerce')

# Convert numeric columns to numeric types, coercing errors to NaN
data['pincode'] = pd.to_numeric(data['pincode'], errors='coerce')
data['bio_age_5_17'] = pd.to_numeric(data['bio_age_5_17'], errors='coerce')
data['bio_age_17_'] = pd.to_numeric(data['bio_age_17_'], errors='coerce')

# Convert numeric columns to pandas' nullable integer type (Int64)
# This allows for NaN values in integer columns without converting to float
data['pincode'] = data['pincode'].astype('Int64')
data['bio_age_5_17'] = data['bio_age_5_17'].astype('Float64')
data['bio_age_17_'] = data['bio_age_17_'].astype('Float64')

print("Data types after conversion:")
data.info()
print("\nNumber of nulls introduced/retained after conversion:")
display(data.isnull().sum())
Data types after conversion: <class 'pandas.core.frame.DataFrame'> RangeIndex: 151534 entries, 0 to 151533 Data columns (total 8 columns): # Column Non-Null Count Dtype --- ------ -------------- ----- 0 date 133553 non-null datetime64[ns] 1 state 151534 non-null object 2 district 151534 non-null object 3 pincode 151534 non-null Int64 4 bio_age_5_17 151533 non-null Float64 5 bio_age_17_ 151533 non-null Float64 6 region_type 151533 non-null object 7 india_region 151111 non-null object dtypes: Float64(2), Int64(1), datetime64[ns](1), object(4) memory usage: 9.7+ MB Number of nulls introduced/retained after conversion:
0
